source: Republic of Korea Ministry of National Defense
note: South Korean government official figures; some historians estimate higher totals including those listed missing in action.

source: US Department of Defense / DoD Korean War statistics
note: US figures are well-documented. Other UN Command nations contributed ~17,000 additional dead. Total UN KIA approximately 54,000.

category: Korean War — Korean People's Army / DPRK (1950–1953)
source: US Department of Defense estimates; various academic studies
note: DPRK does not publish casualty figures. Estimates vary widely from 180,000 to 500,000 KPA dead. Includes those killed by UN air campaign.

category: Korean War — Chinese People's Volunteer Army (1950–1953)
source: PRC official figures (2010 revised) / RAND Corporation
note: PRC officially revised figures upward in 2010. Earlier official figure was 148,000. US estimates ranged 400,000–600,000 killed. Remains politically sensitive in China.

category: Korean War — South Korean Civilian Casualties
source: Republic of Korea Ministry of Health and Social Affairs (1952 survey)
note: Includes deaths from bombardment, atrocities by both sides, and displacement. Some estimates reach 1–2 million. Figures from 1952 survey are considered underestimates.

category: Korean War — North Korean Civilian Casualties
source: US Strategic Bombing Survey / academic estimates
note: Extensive US air campaign destroyed 78 North Korean cities and 85% of Pyongyang. No official DPRK figures. Estimates range 500,000–2M civilian dead from bombardment and displacement.

source: WFP / UN OCHA / academic studies (Haggard & Noland 2007)
note: Range reflects deep uncertainty: official DPRK figure never published. Lower-bound (240,000) from demographic modelling; upper-bound (3.5M) from defector testimonies. Widely cited academic range is 600,000–1M excess deaths.

source: ROK Joint Investigation Group / UN Security Council
note: ROK/US/UK/Australia Joint Investigative Group concluded North Korea sank the corvette with a torpedo. DPRK categorically denied involvement. Russia and China disputed findings.

source: ROK Ministry of National Defense / Yonhap News
note: DPRK fired ~170 artillery shells onto Yeonpyeong Island, killing 2 ROK marines and 2 civilians. South Korea returned fire. First direct attack on civilian territory since the Korean War armistice.

source: UN Commission of Inquiry on Human Rights in DPRK (2014)
note: UN COI (2014) documented systematic extermination, torture, rape, forced labour and starvation in camps. Current camp population estimated at 80,000–120,000. Cumulative death toll derived from defector testimony and satellite imagery. DPRK denies camps exist.

category: DPRK Troops — Combat Casualties in Russia/Ukraine (2024–present)
source: South Korean NIS / Ukrainian military intelligence, Apr 2025
note: South Korean and Ukrainian intelligence reported significant DPRK casualties from fighting in Kursk Oblast. DPRK has not acknowledged deployment; exact figures unverifiable.
